response: Test variants allow you to create a single assessment that adapts to different candidate profiles. You can design multiple variants within the same assessment to personalize the evaluation based on roles, skills, or other criteria. This approach eliminates the need to manage separate tests, improves efficiency, and ensures candidates are assessed on the most relevant content. To create test variants, log in to your HackerRank for Work account, go to the Tests tab, open the test you want to modify, go to the Questions tab, click the more option menu (⋮) in the top right corner, and select Create Variants.
justification: The user is asking about best practices for when to create a new test versus create a variant of the test. The provided support documentation includes a section on 'Create Test Variants' which explains how to create test variants and their benefits. [Source: data/hackerrank/screen/managing-tests/7530103378-test-variants.md] Ticket contains multiple requests; primary issue addressed, secondary requests may require follow-up.

response: To add extra time for a candidate, log in to your HackerRank for Work account and go to the Tests tab. Select the test you want to modify and go to the Candidates tab. Select the checkbox next to the candidate(s) and click More > Add Time. Enter the time in minutes and click Confirm. Please note that a candidate who is still in a test session must refresh the page to see the updated timer.
justification: The user requested step-by-step instructions on how to reinvite a candidate and add extra time. The relevant documentation was found in 'data/hackerrank/screen/managing-tests/4811403281-adding-extra-time-for-candidates.md'. response: You can delete the conversation that contains private information. Once deleted, the indexed data for that conversation will be removed within one hour. Please note that Claude only accesses your data when you explicitly ask a question or request an action requiring this information, and retrieved data is stored on Anthropic servers, protected by Anthropic's security infrastructure. This data is retained with its associated chat, so you can delete any retrieved data by deleting the chat.
